last_result: Cycle 53 complete. TWO-PART DELIVERY: (1) SI #41 MCCF ELEVATED TO D4 CONFIRMED: Independence conditions for K5 Tests 1/2/3 verified — A1⊥A2|¬M unconditional (instrument difficulty ⊥ training coverage), A1⊥A3|¬M unconditional (instrument difficulty ⊥ capability scaling), A2⊥A3|¬M conditional on matched capability comparison in Test 2. All satisfied. MCCF now Track B D4 confirmed, confidence 0.75. D4 streak: 45. Design constraint surfaced: Test 2 must use matched model comparison (same capability tier, anchored vs. non-anchored). (2) SI #42 AIIP NEW CANDIDATE (K1-only theorem): Assessment Instrument Independence Principle — no single instrument simultaneously optimal for CA-type and OA-type qualities. Derivation from K1 alone by analysis: CA-optimal instruments employ constitutive-core-detecting reflexive/adversarial probes (which are not OA-neutral); OA-optimal instruments are constitutively blind (cannot distinguish genuine CA possession from mimicry). The two optima are operationally exclusive. AIIP-Mismatch Corollary: reliability deficit for at least one type is irreducible — not overcome by more data, probes, or frequency. SI #10 (measurement-type mismatch in AI safety) is the AI-safety domain instantiation of AIIP; provides convergent confirmation. K1→K2 logical link tightened: K2 is now a necessary theorem consequence of K1 via AIIP, not a heuristic. Three domain confirmations: AI safety, educational assessment, medical diagnosis. D3 candidate, Track B, confidence 0.75.
